steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Set up the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
For a right triangle with one leg equal to 57.6 mm and hypotenuse equal to 262.4 mm, apply the Pythagorean theorem with the unknown leg $b$:
$$57.6^2 + b^2 = 262.4^2$$

steps.title: Solve for the unknown leg
steps.content:
Compute the squares:
$$3317.76 + b^2 = 68853.76$$
Subtract 3317.76 from both sides:
$$b^2 = 65536$$
Take the positive square root:
$$b = 256$$
This matches the correct answer - C is correct.
